title: Burst Balloons
descriptionHtml:
You are given n balloons, indexed from 0 to n - 1. Each balloon is painted with a number on it represented by an array nums. You are asked to burst all the balloons.

If you burst the ith balloon, you will get nums[i - 1] * nums[i] * nums[i + 1] coins. If i - 1 or i + 1 goes out of bounds of the array, then treat it as if there is a balloon with a 1 painted on it.

Return the maximum coins you can collect by bursting the balloons wisely.

 

Example 1:

Input: nums = [3,1,5,8]
Output: 167
Explanation:
nums = [3,1,5,8] --> [3,5,8] --> [3,8] --> [8] --> []
coins =  3*1*5    +   3*5*8   +  1*3*8  + 1*8*1 = 167

Example 2:

Input: nums = [1,5]
Output: 10

 

Constraints:

  • n == nums.length
  • 1 <= n <= 300
  • 0 <= nums[i] <= 100

canonicalSolutions.code:
# Time:  O(n^3)
# Space: O(n^2)


class Solution(object):
    def maxCoins(self, nums):
        """
        :type nums: List[int]
        :rtype: int
        """
        coins = [1] + [i for i in nums if i > 0] + [1]
        n = len(coins)
        max_coins = [[0 for _ in xrange(n)] for _ in xrange(n)]

        for k in xrange(2, n):
            for left in xrange(n - k):
                right = left + k
                for i in xrange(left + 1, right):
                    max_coins[left][right] = \
                        max(max_coins[left][right],
                            coins[left] * coins[i] * coins[right] +
                            max_coins[left][i] +
                            max_coins[i][right])

        return max_coins[0][-1]
